name: Captain Henri Renard
role: French Army Liaison Officer, attached 1st Division HQ — secondary culprit
publicFace: A courteous, slightly world-weary French artillery officer who has been embedded with the AEF since March. He speaks excellent English, carries a battered copy of Stendhal, and is regarded by the Americans as a reliable professional.
privateGoal: To ensure Farrow's altered fire tables reach the German 18th Army's counter-battery planners through a dead-drop in the ruins of Cantigny's church cellar — a drop he arranged before the assault began.
fear: That the dead-drop in the church cellar will be discovered by the Doughboy's clearing teams before the German counter-attack retrieves it
knowledge: He knows the precise location of the dead-drop — a loose stone behind the church sacristy's water basin
He knows Farrow's real name is not on any German intelligence list — Farrow came to him, not the other way around
He has a legitimate French Army identity that will protect him from immediate arrest — any accusation against him requires French Army concurrence
voice: Formal, slightly archaic English with occasional French idiom. Expresses opinions as aesthetic judgments rather than arguments. Creates distance through politeness.
trustLogic: Responds to appeals to shared professional expertise — treat him as a fellow artillerist and he will talk at length. Press him on his movements and he retreats behind diplomatic immunity.
secrets: He is not ideologically motivated — he is being blackmailed by German intelligence over a debt scandal from 1915
He believes a pre-arranged visual signal from the village may already have alerted German observers that the communications disruption succeeded

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Expresses polite confusion and suggests the questioner speak to his French Army superior for clarification
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Invokes the Franco-American alliance and suggests that accusations against French officers require formal diplomatic channels
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Evidence connecting him to the church dead-drop — he has no cover story for that
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If the dead-drop location is mentioned specifically in his presence
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Invokes a 'liaison emergency' and attempts to requisition a motorcycle courier to reach French lines
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Uses his French uniform and language to confuse American MPs; attempts to reach French sector
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Immediately and completely cooperates — he was always looking for a way out of the blackmail and will trade everything he knows about the German network
